name: Minnie Pearl
abstract: Sarah Ophelia Colley Cannon (October 25, 1912 – March 4, 1996), known professionally as her stage character Minnie Pearl, was an American comedian and country singer who appeared at the Grand Ole Opry for more than 50 years (1940–1991) and on the television show Hee Haw from 1969 to 1991.

concerts.label: Ernest Tubb and Grand Ole Opry Stars
Ernest Tubb and Grand Ole Opry Stars

about_quotes.speaker: David N. Baker
about_quotes.evidence: I knew Minnie Pearl and Gene Autry and everybody else of the singing cowboys.
about_quotes.doc_title: David N. Baker — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Magnarelli
about_quotes.evidence: So he brought in another — her name was Minnie Pearl. And she was an established organ player in Syracuse. So she came in, and plus they used an organ. They couldn’t do a piano.
